presentation.latex: ⊘. Concretely, the children of a state s = (s1,..., sD) are vectors that can be obtained from s by changing any one entry sd from ⊘ to 0 or 1, and its parents are states that can be obtained by changing a single entry sd ∈ {0, 1} to ⊘. We deﬁne|s| ≜ #{sd | sd ∈ { 0, 1},d = 1,...,D }, the number of non-void entries in s, so X is naturally identiﬁed
